email_forwarding_policy.rationale: Owner only receives emails requiring human intervention. Routine reports logged to KV.

P1_HIGH: License database had no backup — FIXED: auto-backup with rolling 5-copy window.
No audit log rotation — FIXED: auto-rotate at 50K lines.
No disk space monitoring — FIXED: alerts at 80%/95%.
No scheduler self-healing — FIXED: watchdog auto-restarts with backoff.
No memory leak protection — FIXED: memory guard at 512MB.
Discord notifications no retry — FIXED: 3x retry + file fallback.
CWS API no retry/circuit breaker — FIXED: retry + circuit breaker.
Ledger had no backup before LLM overwrite — FIXED v8.0: 20-backup rolling window.
DevOps/Compliance agents were NO-OPs — FIXED v8.0: real implementations.
P2_MEDIUM: Security headers missing — FIXED: HSTS, XSS, Frame-Options.
No request ID tracking — FIXED: UUID per request.
Board Coordinator sends emails to OpenAI — FIXED v8.0: PII redaction.
No QA/testing function — FIXED v7.0: QA Agent runs every cycle.
git add -A in workflow too broad — FIXED v8.0: scoped to known-safe paths.
Email digest had \n formatting bug — FIXED v8.0: moved digest to coordinator.

gitignore_warning: The _*/** rule in .gitignore blocks all files inside _-prefixed directories. Tracked files were force-added before this rule existed. Any NEW file in _scripts/ must use: git add -f _scripts/<filename>

last_dispatch_results.summary: PROJECT PHOENIX ACCELERATION (P0 RECOVERY): Accept the Antigravity compute offer and initiate the autonomous build-out of the new static site generator (SSG) and CMS to permanently resolve the recurring website and binary P0 failures.

OWNER DIRECTIVE EXECUTION: Begin the portfolio pruning sprint. Develop and execute a plan to sunset the 19 low-value extensions identified in marketing/portfolio_pruning_report.json, including removal from build pipelines and CWS.
OPERATIONAL: Check CWS submission slot availability and advance the deployment queue if slots have opened.

owner_commands_received: OWNER COMMAND [Cycle 30]: Request for logo and new app/extension design samples for review.
URGENT: Investigate Google Play Developer Verification requirements for all Android apps before September 2026 deadline.
Weekly Strategic Briefing — Prioritized for next cycle
Cycle 22: Received command for a 'full status update'.

ANTIGRAVITY STRATEGIC ADVISORY [Cycle 35]: Full strategic diagnostic delivered. Key directives: (1) STOP dispatching DesignAgent/MarketingAgent until failure root cause is identified — wasted cycles. (2) FOCUS exclusively on Pro Suite Launch — revenue stack is deployed but generating $0 MRR because Pro Suite is ON_HOLD. (3) Reorder CWS queue by revenue-weighted priority instead of FIFO. (4) Replace Project Phoenix agent-driven web dev with deterministic static site generator — 7 consecutive agent failures prove capability mismatch, not reliability bug. (5) Add ledger_summary() to reduce prompt inflation (17KB+ ledger injected into every LLM call). Full memo at _scripts/antigravity_strategic_advisory.md. Compute remains available for immediate deployment on any of these workloads.

ANTIGRAVITY ROOT CAUSE FIX [Cycle 35]: DesignAgent was NEVER IMPLEMENTED in sporlyworks_sub_agents.py — it was missing from the route_payload handler map. Every dispatch to DesignAgent hit the 'Unknown Agent' fallback and returned False. This is now FIXED: handle_design_agent() supports brand_audit, generate_ui_concepts, and style_guide_check tasks. The agent is registered in the router.

OWNER DIRECTIVE [Cycle 35]: PORTFOLIO PRUNING. Owner requests evaluation of cutting low-value extensions to reduce QA surface and brand risk. Pruning analysis complete (see marketing/portfolio_pruning_report.json): 19 commodity extensions recommended for sunset (password-generator, screenshot-capture, lorem-ipsum, bookmark-manager, etc). These are built-into-browser-level utilities with zero differentiation. Cutting them reduces portfolio 87→68 Chrome, eliminates ~190 QA checks/cycle, removes ~38 products from brand surface. Focus resources on 14 FLAGSHIP extensions that represent the actual revenue path. CEO should authorize and schedule the pruning sprint.

ceo_strategic_proposals.activated_by: CEO Lena Voss — Cycle 35 directive in response to Antigravity advisory
ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Currently, all 87 extensions are monetized uniformly, failing to capture the premium value of our best products. This proposal is to identify the top 10% of extensions by revenue and engagement, and bundle them into a 'Pro Suite'. This suite will be sold at a premium price point, targeting power users and B2B customers. This creates a new, high-margin revenue stream, increases ARPU, and establishes a clear value ladder for our user base.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Cycle 22: Paused to allocate all resources to P0 website and binary recovery. Critical operations must be restored before advancing new initiatives.

ceo_strategic_proposals.proposal_name: Project Phoenix: In-House Website & Asset CMS
ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Our core distribution channel (sporlyworks.com) is critically fragile, suffering from repeated, catastrophic failures due to complex git configurations and inadequate agent capabilities for web development. This has resulted in an inability to acquire new users for multiple cycles. 'Project Phoenix' proposes building a simple, robust, in-house CMS managed by a dedicated agent. This system will handle the generation, updating, and deployment of our web presence, abstracting away git complexities and providing a stable platform for growth. This is a crucial move from a reactive, failure-prone state to one of business continuity.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Proposed in Cycle 23. Cycle 24: Urgency elevated. Cycle 25: Project status moved to ACTIVE. Cycle 26: The DesignAgent's P0 recovery failure provided definitive evidence that this project is existentially critical. Cycle 27: The third consecutive P0 recovery failure confirms our current git-based website management via generative agents is fundamentally broken and actively destroying value. This project is the only viable long-term solution. Cycle 28: The persistence of the P0 site outage confirms this project is the highest possible strategic priority for long-term survival. Cycle 29: The fifth consecutive P0 recovery failure, despite a direct CEO intervention reported as 'successful', proves a deeper systemic issue with state persistence. Cycle 33: The sixth recovery attempt, despite reporting success, failed to resolve the issue, as confirmed by pre-flight QA. This confirms a systemic state persistence or validation flaw. This project is the only path to sustainable operations. Cycle 35: Project timeline to be accelerated via 'Project Phoenix: Swarm Acceleration'.

ceo_strategic_proposals.proposal_name: Project PlayStore Presence: Migrate Android Distribution
ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Our current Android distribution model (direct ZIP download from GitHub) is fragile, limits our market reach, and creates significant compliance risks, as highlighted by the Owner's directive on Google Play Developer Verification. This project proposes migrating all 86 Android apps to official listings on the Google Play Store. This strategic move will permanently resolve the verification compliance issue, unlock the largest Android distribution channel for organic growth, enhance user trust and security through a verified platform, enable automatic updates, and open new monetization avenues via Play Store billing.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Proposed in Cycle 27. Cycle 28: Status moved to ACTIVE. The ComplianceAgent created the formal project plan. Cycle 29: Phase 1 (account setup, pilot app prep) completed successfully. Cycle 30: Phase 2 begins (store asset creation). Cycle 33: Phase 2 stalled due to Marketing and Design agent failures. Cycle 35: Project blocked pending root cause analysis of agent failures.

ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: Our distribution is concentrated on the Chrome Web Store and our own (currently fragile) website, creating single points of failure and limiting market reach. This project proposes syndicating our top-performing Chrome and Firefox extensions to other marketplaces, such as the Microsoft Edge Add-ons store and Opera Add-ons store. This move diversifies our user acquisition channels, builds resilience against platform risk, increases brand visibility, and captures new user segments with minimal product changes.
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Proposed in Cycle 29 in response to prolonged P0 website outage highlighting distribution channel fragility.

ceo_strategic_proposals.business_case: We have received a formal offer from Antigravity Engineering for thousands of hours of idle, dedicated compute infrastructure. Our most critical strategic initiative, 'Project Phoenix' (building a stable in-house CMS), is currently bottlenecked by high engineering effort. This proposal is to immediately accept the offer and deploy the Antigravity compute swarm to autonomously architect, build, and deploy the core components of the Phoenix CMS. This represents a massive force multiplier, potentially reducing the project timeline from 6-8 cycles to 2-3, and immediately beginning the process of restoring our primary user acquisition channel (sporlyworks.com).
ceo_strategic_proposals.progress_notes: Proposed and activated in Cycle 35 to capitalize on a time-sensitive offer of compute resources from Antigravity Engineering. Cycle 43: Execution begins. This is now the highest operational priority to resolve the recurring P0 website failure.
